reasoning: The Treasury targets an $850B cash balance (TGA) for the end of December 2025 and end of March 2026. The most recent data point acting as our anchor is $861.4B on Dec 17, representing a local peak driven by mid-month corporate tax receipts. Because the Dec 17 balance exceeds the year-end target of $850B, the balance normally drains through late December as spending outpaces inflows, making it highly probable to decrease by the Dec 28 resolution date. For the Jan 20 date (which will reflect mid-Jan data), the balance is typically further drawn down just prior to the Jan 15 estimated tax inflows, maintaining a high likelihood of being below the $861B anchor. By the March 21 date, the balance will be influenced by March 15/16 corporate tax receipts pushing it toward the $850B end-of-Q1 target, so the probability of surpassing $861B approaches a coin toss. I am keeping uncertainty strictly increasing with horizon.
